source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/07_Retrofit (HnX-R1)/★ FDA 510(k) - K252912/NSE에 따른 PRE-SUB진행 (1차)/Clinical report/000_Clinical Image Test Plan.docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical image acquisition and evaluation protocol for HnX-R1 Digital X-ray Imaging System to support substantial equivalence to legally marketed predicate devices.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used as a clinical image test plan for FDA 510(k) evidence, referencing Section VII Clinical Considerations of FDA guidance for solid state X-ray imaging devices.
keyPoints: HnX-R1 consists of wired/wireless detectors, power supply box, battery pack, battery charger, accessories, and HnVUE image acquisition and processing software.
The device is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of adult and pediatric human anatomy and is intended to replace film-based radiographic diagnostic systems.
The device is not to be used for mammography.
Clinical image evaluation is to be performed by a clinical expert, identified as radiologist Wonho Chang.
Predicate devices are identified by K223930 and K213497.

evidence.claim: The document is titled Clinical Image Test Plan.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical Image Test Plan

evidence.claim: The subject product is a Digital X-ray Imaging System with model name HnX-R1.
evidence.evidenceText:
Product name: Digital X-ray Imaging System

Model name: HnX-R1

evidence.claim: The clinical image test plan is intended to support substantial equivalence for the Retrofit Kit of X-ray System HnX-R1.
evidence.evidenceText: The purpose of this Clinical Image Test Plan is to establish a comprehensive protocol for the acquisition and evaluation of clinical images to demonstrate that the Retrofit Kit of X-ray System (HnX-R1) is substantially equivalent to a legally marketed predicate device.

evidence.claim: The plan references FDA 510(k) guidance for solid state X-ray imaging devices.
evidence.evidenceText: Section VII Clinical Considerations of “Guidance for the Submission of 510(k)s for Solid State X-ray Imaging Devices.”
evidence.pageTarget: FDA 510(k) Guidance for Solid State X-ray Imaging Devices

evidence.claim: HnX-R1 includes detectors, power supply box, battery pack, battery charger, accessories, and software, and does not include the X-ray generator.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-R1 wired/wireless digital X-ray imaging system consists of Detector, Power supply box, Battery pack, Battery charger, Other Accessories and software for image acquisition and image processing and does not include the X-ray generator.

evidence.claim: The G series detector models are G1417CW, G1417CWP, G1417FW, G1417FWP, G1717CW, G1717CWP, G1717FW, and G1717FWP.
evidence.evidenceText: there are 8 types of G series (G1417CW, G1417CWP, G1417FW, G1417FWP, G1717CW, G1717CWP, G1717FW, G1717FWP)

evidence.claim: The 2/3G series detector models are A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: and 3 types of 2/3G series (A1717MCW, A1417MCW, F1417MCW).

evidence.claim: The console software is HnVUE.
evidence.evidenceText: The name of the image acquisition and image processing software (Console software) is HnVUE.

evidence.claim: HnX-R1 is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of adult and pediatric human anatomy.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-R1(Digital X-ray Imaging System) is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: HnX-R1 is intended to replace film-based radiographic diagnostic systems.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic system.

evidence.claim: HnX-R1 is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: HnVUE software version is 1.0.0.
evidence.evidenceText:
Software name (version)

: HnVUE (1.0.0)

evidence.claim: HnVUE supports DICOM 3.0 image formats, transmission, and storage.
evidence.evidenceText: Image formats, transmission, and storage supported by the DICOM 3.0 standard

evidence.claim: The test procedure includes patient registration, anatomical region selection, subject positioning, X-ray preparation, image verification, DICOM export, and clinical expert evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText:
1) Patient Register

2) Selection of Anatomical Regions

3) Positioning the subject on the detector

4) Preparation for X-ray

5) X-ray selection parameter and shot

6) Image verification (Post processing)

7) Export image (DICOM)

8) Clinical image evaluation by clinical expert

reviewFlags.reason: Document date is not present; prepared, review, and approval date fields are blank in the source text.
Revision history shows Revision No. 0 but no revision date or revision description.
Authority is inferred from the source metadata authoritySignal and FDA 510(k) guidance reference; the document itself does not identify a submitting authority header.
Preprocessed table text contains formatting and OCR artifacts, including split table rows and 'M iddle', which may affect detailed exposure parameter extraction.
Section numbering appears inconsistent: console software is listed as '3.2.2' after detector section '3.3.1'.
